content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand my role and the current state of the project.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover  (iteration 069 — **open third parallel track for Phase C (Jacobian construction)** alongside continuing Phase A Čech closure and Phase B differentials. Iter-066 through iter-068 delivered minimal net closure: BasicOpenCech remained at 15 syntactic sorries, Differentials at 7. User directive 2026-05-12 explicitly overrides the previous deferral of `Jacobian.lean` and asks for the best viable route given existing infrastructure. Iter-069 dispatches three parallel prover objectives.)
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). After iter-068, Phase A's `BasicOpenCech.lean` has **15 syntactic sorries** in `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`; Phase B's `Differentials.lean` has **7 syntactic sorries**; Phase C's `Jacobian.lean` has **5 protected sorries** now in active scope; Phase D's `Genus.lean` is closed; Phase E's `AbelJacobi.lean` has **3 protected sorries** deferred until `Jacobian C` lands.
18	
19	## Iteration 069 plan-agent summary (this iteration)
20	
21	**Iter-068 verification.** Plan-agent verification this pass:
22	
23	- **Sorry count (syntactic)**: `31 total across 5 file(s)` via `sorry_analyzer`: 15 `BasicOpenCech.lean` + 7 `Differentials.lean` + 5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`. Iter-066 through iter-068 made no net reduction on the main tracks. The previous plan-agent count from iter-065 of `30` is revised to `31` because `Differentials.lean` independently counts 7 syntactic sorries (the iter-065 plan-agent labelled-substep convention undercounted a nested `by sorry` inside `cotangentExactSeq_structure`).
24	- **File LOC**: `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` 834 LOC (unchanged since iter-065). `Differentials.lean` 449 LOC (was 353, +96 from iter-066–068 prover additions; the plan-agent will verify exact delta after the prover round).
25	- **`Genus.lean`**: 0 sorries, kernel-only.
26	- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: unchanged.
27	- **No new axioms** introduced by iter-066–068 prover rounds.
28	- **`lean_diagnostic_messages`**: errors empty on all files; expected `declaration uses 'sorry'` warnings.
29	- **USER_HINTS.md**: user directive 2026-05-12 read and incorporated into this iteration's plan. File will be cleared after this write.
30	
31	**Iter-069 work scope.**
32	
33	Three parallel prover objectives:
34	
35	1. **`Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — continue closing sorries inside `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`.** The concrete `π : K ⟶ K₀` construction from iter-065 is load-bearing. Iter-066–068 provers made no net closure. Iter-069 returns the prover with the same prioritized targets:
36	   - `h_π_split (i : ℕ) : SplitEpi (π.f i)` at L787 — the splitting is now a well-typed obligation. The helper `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` (L362) reduces this to identifying `π.f i` with a `Pi.lift` form. After `simp only [π, NatTrans.hcomp_app, ...]` the goal contains `𝟙 X ≫ f.f i`; the prover should complete the identification and apply the helper.
37	   - `h_mod_X₁/X₂/X₃` at L887-889 — `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on the cochain factors of `K₀`. Each factor is a finite product (index `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀`) of restrictions `Γ(C.left, V_x)` with `V_x ≤ U`; construct the instance via `Pi.module` after transporting along `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`.
38	   - Original substep (a) at L495 — extra-degeneracy on the `s`-indexed slice cover via `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech → ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv → CochainComplex.opEquivalence`.
39	   - Or any of the other transient internal sorries (L817 kernel-LES, L896-899 `f_R/g_R/hf_eq/hg_eq`, L905-910 `h_loc_X₁/₂/₃`, L916 `h_loc_exact`).
40	
41	2. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — attack `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`/`Beta` or `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` substep 2.** The iter-065 refactor decomposed `cotangent_exact_sequence` into named `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`, and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` substeps. Iter-069 prover should prioritize whichever they judge most tractable:
42	   - **Substep 2 of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` (L113)** — the sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens of an affine open. This is the scheme-language version of `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`: for `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Globalise via `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafFor` / restrict-to-basis machinery.
43	   - **`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L199)** — the base-change cotangent map `f^* Ω_{Y/S} → Ω_{X/S}`. Gluing the local `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange` maps into a global `Scheme.Modules` morphism using `PresheafOfModules.homMk`.
44	   - **`cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L218)** — the relative-quotient cotangent map `Ω_{X/S} → Ω_{X/Y}`. Gluing the local `KaehlerDifferential.map` maps into a global morphism.
45	
46	3. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — BEGIN Phase C construction.** The user directive 2026-05-12 explicitly opens a third parallel track. The `PicardFunctor.representable` route remains gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (absent from Mathlib). The best viable alternative is the **Albanese-functor representability framework** combined with **symmetric-power infrastructure**:
47	   - Define the Albanese functor: for a pointed curve `(C, P)`, the functor sending a pointed abelian variety `(A, a)` over `k` to the set of pointed morphisms `C → A` sending `P` to `a`.
48	   - Show that a representing object for this functor would simultaneously deliver `Jacobian C` (the representing scheme), `ofCurve P` (the universal pointed morphism), `comp_ofCurve` (pointed property), and `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (universal property).
49	   - Decompose the 5 remaining sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into smaller, named substeps with clear mathematical content.
50	   - Build helper definitions as needed (e.g., `ConnectedComponentOfIdentity` for group schemes, `IsAlbanese` predicate).
51	   - The iter-069 goal is **scaffolding and decomposition**, not full closure. Transient sorries are explicitly acceptable.
52	   - Do NOT use forbidden shortcuts (terminal object `Spec k`, projective space `P^g`, constant-zero maps).
53	
54	## Current Objectives
55	
56	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`** — close one or more sorries inside `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
57	
58	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers*.
59	
60	Iter-065 prover report (**recommended pre-read**): `logs/iter-065/prover-BasicOpenCech-result.md` — contains the concrete construction of `π : K ⟶ K₀` (lines 736–754 of the Lean file), explanation of the four-step Mathlib functor chain, and detailed next-step recommendations.
61	
62	**Action**: Engage with the body of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` (L453). The proof contains 15 syntactic sorries organised by substep. Preferred targets (easiest wins per iter-065 prover):
63	
64	- **L787** — `h_π_split (i : ℕ) : SplitEpi (π.f i)`: the helper `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` (L362) is already defined and closed. The remaining work is to identify `π.f i` with the `Pi.lift` form after the `simp` block at L796. Complete the simp identification and apply the helper.
65	- **L887-889** — `h_mod_X₁/X₂/X₃ : Module R scK₀.Xᵢ`: install `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on cochain factors of `K₀`. Via `Pi.module` + `ModuleCat.piIsoPi` transport.
66	- **L495** — substep (a): extra-degeneracy on the `s`-indexed slice cover.
67	
68	The prover should **prioritize whichever sorry they judge most tractable**.
69	
70	#### Notes for the prover
71	
72	- **File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` only. Do not modify any other `.lean` file.
73	- **Mathematical content**: in the blueprint chapter `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`, in `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, and in the iter-060/061/062/063/064/065 prover task reports.
74	- **Existing scaffolding in scope**: all iter-016 → iter-065 declarations now live across the three split files; the iter-065 concrete `π` construction at L736-754 is the foundation for `h_π_split` and the kernel-LES argument. The `hs_fin` `have` block is at L609-611. The `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` helper at L362 is closed and ready to use.
75	- **Transient sorries are explicitly acceptable.** If a sorry exceeds the session, leave a well-commented `sorry` naming it and any sub-components.
76	- **Internal helper lemmas / `noncomputable def`s are explicitly encouraged.**
77	- **Do NOT revert** the iter-065 concrete `π` construction (L736-754) — it is load-bearing for any downstream LES manipulation.
78	
79	#### What success looks like
80	
81	**(Best)** One or more sorries fully closed. Syntactic-sorry trajectory `15 → 14` or better. Closing `h_π_split` or L495 would be highest impact.
82	
83	**(Good)** Substantial new scaffolding landed (e.g., `Pi.module` instances drafted with explicit transport; `ExtraDegeneracy` instance constructed for substep (a); short-exact-sequence `ker(π) → K → K₀` packaged as a `ShortComplex`). Trajectory unchanged or +1 from finer decomposition.
84	
85	**(Acceptable)** A sorry decomposed into finer substeps with clear commentary. Iter-070 pickup is unambiguous.
86	
87	**(Failure)** No engagement beyond iter-065: all 15 sorries unchanged, no new scaffolding.
88	
89	### 2. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean`** — attack `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`, or `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` substep 2
90	
91	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
92	
93	Iter-065 prover report (**recommended pre-read**): `logs/iter-065/prover-Differentials-result.md` — contains the `moduleKPresheafOfModules` cluster fix details, the `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` decomposition, and iter-066 next-step recommendations.
94	
95	**Action**: Attack one of the three highest-priority closures:
96	
97	1. **`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L199)** — the base-change cotangent map `f^* Ω_{Y/S} → Ω_{X/S}`. For a composition `X → Y → S`, on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`, `A' = O_Y(f(V))`, `B = O_X(V)`, this is `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`: `Ω_{A'/A} ⊗_{A'} B → Ω_{B/A}`. The global construction glues these local maps using `PresheafOfModules.homMk` applied to the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange`.
98	
99	2. **`cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L218)** — the relative-quotient cotangent map `Ω_{X/S} → Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B`: `Ω_{B/A} → Ω_{B/A'}`. The global construction follows from the morphism of `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'` induced by the natural transformation `(f ≫ g)⁻¹ O_S → f⁻¹ O_Y` between the pullback ring presheaves.
100	
101	3. **Substep 2 of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` (L113)** — A scheme-language localisation-compatibility lemma. For an affine open `Spec B ↪ X` with structure ring map `A → B` (induced by the morphism `f : X → S`), and for each `g ∈ B`, the sections of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over the basic open `D(g)` are isomorphic to `Ω_{B[1/g]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/g]`. Mathlib leverage: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic`), `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (the ring-map version). The sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens then follows by descent. **This is a multi-iteration effort (~200-400 LOC).** Iter-069 may land scaffolding + helper lemmas + draft a localisation-compatibility theorem for one basic open without fully closing the sheaf condition; transient sorries are acceptable.
102	
103	The prover may also opportunistically attack other sorries (`smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, `cotangent_at_section`, `serre_duality_genus`) if the session permits, but **items 1–3 are the priority**.
104	
105	#### Notes for the prover
106	
107	- **File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` only. Do not modify any other `.lean` file.
108	- **Mathematical content**: in `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
109	- **Mathlib leverage**:
110	  - `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic`) — ring-level base-change cotangent map.
111	  - `KaehlerDifferential.map` — ring-level relative-quotient cotangent map.
112	  - `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.CotangentComplex`) — the ring-level cotangent exact sequence (composition zero, exactness, surjectivity).
113	  - `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` / `isLocalizedModule_map` — localisation compatibility.
114	  - `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'` — the presheaf-of-modules differential construction already used in `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`.
115	  - `PresheafOfModules.homMk` — for constructing global module homomorphisms from compatible local data.
116	- **Constraints**: do not introduce new `axiom` declarations. The file may compile with `sorry` bodies.

129	### 3. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean`** — BEGIN Phase C: scaffold the Albanese-functor / symmetric-power construction route for `Jacobian C`
130	
131	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex`.
132	
133	**Context**: The five protected sorries in `Jacobian.lean` (`Jacobian` L77, `instGrpObj` L88, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` L97, `instIsProper` L104, `instGeometricallyIrreducible` L114) have been deferred since iter-001 because they reduce to the Picard-scheme representability theorem `PicardFunctor.representable`, which is gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (absent from Mathlib) and the FGA argument. The user directive 2026-05-12 explicitly overrides this deferral and asks for the best viable route given existing project infrastructure.
134	
135	**The best viable route**: the **Albanese universal property** combined with the **symmetric-power / divisor-class** construction.
136	
137	Mathematically, for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k` with a `k`-rational point `P`, the Jacobian `Jac(C)` is the abelian variety that represents the functor
138	
139	```
140	T ↦ { pointed morphisms C_T → A_T over T | P ↦ 0 }
141	```
142	
143	for variable abelian varieties `A` over `k`. Equivalently, `Jac(C)` is the connected component of the identity of the Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}`, which parametrizes degree-0 line bundles on `C`.
144	
145	Since the Picard-scheme route is blocked, the prover should begin the **Albanese-functor route**:
146	
147	1. **Define the Albanese functor** `AlbaneseFunctor C P : (Over (Spec k))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` that sends a `k`-scheme `T` to the set of pairs `(A, f)` where `A` is an abelian variety over `T` and `f : C_T → A` is a morphism sending the `T`-point induced by `P` to the identity section of `A`. (Or, more simply, define it as the functor of pointed morphisms to a fixed abelian variety and then vary the abelian variety.)
148	
149	   A more tractable first step: define a predicate `IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec k)) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec k)) [GrpObj J] : Prop` that says `J` satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. This predicate bundles the data of `ofCurve P : C ⟶ J` and the universal property `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`.
150	
151	2. **Show that `IsAlbanese` characterizes `J` uniquely up to isomorphism.** If `IsAlbanese C P J₁` and `IsAlbanese C P J₂`, then there is a unique isomorphism `J₁ ≅ J₂` compatible with the Abel–Jacobi maps. This uses the rigidity theorem (`AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean`, closed iter-002).
152	
153	3. **Attempt to construct `Jacobian C` from `IsAlbanese`.** If the prover can prove `∃ J, IsAlbanese C P J`, then `Jacobian C` can be defined as `Classical.choose` of this existential. The existence proof would go via the symmetric-power route: for `g = genus C`, the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g(C)` (or the `g`-fold product `C^g` modulo the symmetric group action) maps surjectively to `Pic^g(C)` via the Abel–Jacobi map; tensoring with the fixed line bundle `O(gP)^{-1}` identifies `Pic^g(C) ≅ Pic^0(C) = Jac(C)`.
154	
155	   Since Mathlib does not have symmetric powers of schemes or quotients by finite group actions, the prover should **define the necessary infrastructure**:
156	   - `SymmetricPower C n` as a type (using the `n`-fold product in `Over (Spec k)` plus the symmetric group action, or as a placeholder type with the intended universal property).
157	   - The Abel–Jacobi map `C^g → Pic^g` at the functor-of-points level.
158	   - The identification `Pic^g ≅ Pic^0` via the fixed point `P`.
159	
160	4. **If the full construction is out of reach in one session**, the prover should:
161	   - Define `IsAlbanese` and prove the uniqueness statement.
162	   - Decompose the 5 sorries into smaller, named substeps with clear mathematical commentary.
163	   - Build any helper definitions that will be reusable in subsequent iterations.
164	   - Leave well-commented transient sorries.
165	
166	#### Notes for the prover

169	- **Protected signatures**: read `archon-protected.yaml`. The signatures of `Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, and `instGeometricallyIrreducible` are frozen. You may fill proof bodies but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.

8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.
101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/
117	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen
118	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
119	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
121	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
122	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
123	          IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s) ∧
124	          HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)) :
125	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F where
126	  exists_cover {U} hU := by
127	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hacy, hcomp⟩ := h hU
128	    exact ⟨s, basicOpenCover s, basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top hU s hs, hacy, hcomp⟩
129	
130	/-- Iter-055: curve specialisation of `hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` at
131	`F := toModuleKSheaf C`.
132	
133	Mirrors the iter-039/042/043/048/049/050/051/052/053 `_curve` pattern: a thin
134	dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the
135	structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-055 producer
136	is chained through. Used by the iter-057+ packaging step to instantiate
137	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` once iter-056+ supplies the
138	per-affine basic-open Čech-acyclic + comparison-iso evidence. -/
139	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen_curve
140	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
141	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
142	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
143	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
144	          IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s) ∧
145	          HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)) :
146	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) :=
147	  hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen h
148	
149	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection identification.
150	
151	Given a Set `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` and indices `i j : s`, the intersection of the
152	two basic-open cover members `basicOpenCover s i` and `basicOpenCover s j`
153	equals the basic open of the product `i.1 * j.1`. Direct iteration of Mathlib's
154	`Scheme.basicOpen_mul`.
155	
156	This is the foundational identification for iter-057+ Čech complex evaluation:
157	the Čech cochain at degree 1 indexes pairs `(i, j) : s × s` and assigns the
158	sheaf evaluated at `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j`. Via this lemma,
159	that intersection is identified with `C.left.basicOpen (i.1 * j.1)`, which by
160	`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the localization
161	`Localization.Away (i.1 * j.1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. The Koszul-style
162	alternating-sum exactness on these localizations follows from
163	`exact_of_localized_span` (Mathlib) under `Ideal.span s = ⊤`. -/
164	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul
165	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
166	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
167	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
168	    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j =
169	      C.left.basicOpen ((i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) * j.1) := by
170	  unfold basicOpenCover
171	  exact (Scheme.basicOpen_mul C.left _ _).symm
172	
173	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself
174	affine.
175	
176	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, and indices `i j : s`,
177	the intersection `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j` is itself affine.
178	Thin wrapper combining `basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` (rewriting the
179	intersection as a basic open of the product) with Mathlib's
180	`IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` (membership preservation under basic-open formation).
181	
182	Generalises iter-054's `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen` (single-index affine
183	membership) to pairwise intersections. Used by iter-057+ Čech evaluation: each
184	finite intersection of basic opens is itself affine, which is the inductive
185	step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument (Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5
186	/ Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul resolution). -/
187	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
189	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
190	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
191	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓
192	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j) := by
193	  unfold basicOpenCover
194	  rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
195	  exact hU.basicOpen _
196	
197	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection identification (Finset.inf' form).
198	
199	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
200	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` equals the basic open of the
201	finite product `∏ i ∈ t, i.1` in `Γ(C.left, U)`. Generalises iter-056's binary
202	`basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing.
203	
204	Body via `Finset.cons_induction`: the empty case is impossible by `t.Nonempty`
205	(closed via `Finset.not_nonempty_empty`); the cons case splits on whether the
206	residual `t` is empty or non-empty (via `Finset.eq_empty_or_nonempty`). In the
207	empty residual case, `simp [basicOpenCover]` closes via single-element reduction
208	of `Finset.inf'` and `Finset.prod`. In the non-empty residual case, decompose
209	via `Finset.inf'_cons hne` to expose `basicOpenCover s a ⊓ rest`, decompose
210	`Finset.prod_cons` to expose `a.1 * (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, apply the IH to identify
211	`rest = C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, then `change ... ⊓ _ = _` followed by
212	`rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]` consolidates the product form.
213	
214	This is the foundational identification for iter-058+ Čech complex evaluation
215	at arbitrary degree `n`: the Čech cochain at degree `n` indexes `x : Fin (n+1) → s`
216	and assigns the sheaf evaluated at `⨅ i : Fin (n+1), basicOpenCover s (x i)`.
217	Via this lemma (instantiated at `t := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))).image x`,
218	or more directly via `Finset.univ.inf' Finset.univ_nonempty (basicOpenCover s ∘ x)`),
219	that intersection identifies with `C.left.basicOpen (∏ i : Fin (n+1), (x i).1)`,
220	which by `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the
221	localization `Localization.Away (∏ i, (x i).1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. -/
222	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod
223	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
224	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
225	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
226	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) =
227	      C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U))) := by
228	  classical
229	  induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
230	  | empty => exact absurd h Finset.not_nonempty_empty
231	  | cons a t ha ih =>
232	    rcases t.eq_empty_or_nonempty with rfl | hne
233	    · simp [basicOpenCover]
234	    · rw [Finset.inf'_cons hne, Finset.prod_cons, ih hne]
235	      change C.left.basicOpen (a.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) ⊓ _ = _
236	      rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
237	
238	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself affine.
239	
240	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, `(t : Finset s)`, and
241	`(h : t.Nonempty)`, the n-ary intersection `t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is
242	itself affine. Term-mode body chains
243	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting the n-ary intersection
244	as a basic open of the finite product) with Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
245	(membership preservation under basic-open formation).
246	
247	Generalises iter-056's `basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen` (pairwise affine
248	membership) to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing. Used by iter-058+ Čech
249	evaluation: each finite intersection of basic opens of an affine is itself
250	affine, which is the inductive step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument
251	(Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul
252	resolution).
253	
254	**Body uses `▸` (Eq.mpr), NOT `rw`**: `rw` on the equation produced by Theorem
255	(1) fails because of motive-occurrence issues with the implicit `Subtype.val`
256	coercions on elements of `Finset s`; term-mode `▸` rewrite resolves these
257	directly without invoking the rewriter's pattern-matching. Plan-agent
258	`lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirmed the term-mode form compiles
259	kernel-only while the tactic-mode `rw` form fails with "Did not find an
260	occurrence of the pattern" error. -/
261	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen
262	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
263	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
264	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
265	    IsAffineOpen (t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
266	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸ hU.basicOpen _
267	
268	/-- Iter-058: n-ary basic-open intersection is contained in `U`.
269	
270	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
271	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` is contained in `U`. Term-mode
272	body chains iter-057's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting
273	the n-ary intersection as a single basic open of the finite product) with
274	Mathlib's `Scheme.basicOpen_le` (`X.basicOpen f ≤ U` for any `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`)
275	via Lean's `▸` (Eq.mpr) rewrite.
276	
277	**No `IsAffineOpen` hypothesis required**: `basicOpen_le` holds for any open `U`
278	(the basic open of `f ∈ Γ(X, U)` is always a sub-open of `U`); compare iter-057's
279	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` which does need `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`.
280	
281	This is the inclusion morphism iter-059+ Čech-evaluation arguments need when
282	applying Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`:
283	```
284	(hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
285	  V = X.basicOpen f → IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)
286	```
287	The morphism `i : V ⟶ U` argument (which is `homOfLE` of `V ≤ U`) is needed to
288	install the `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` instance for the conclusion type, since
289	Mathlib's `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires for the
290	syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, not for `V = t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)`.
291	Iter-058's inclusion lemma supplies this `V ≤ U` proof so iter-059+ can call
292	`hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen f (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le ...))
293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land
294	`IsLocalization.Away (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s))`
295	directly. -/
296	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le
297	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
298	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
299	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
300	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) ≤ U :=
301	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸
302	    C.left.basicOpen_le _
303	
304	/-- Iter-059: n-ary basic-open intersection is a localization.
305	
306	Given an affine open `U`, a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, and a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`,
307	the structure sheaf evaluated on the n-ary intersection of basic-open cover members
308	`t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is identified — as a localization away from the product
309	`∏ i ∈ t, i.1` of generators — with `Γ(C.left, U)`. The algebra structure on the conclusion
310	type is given explicitly by `(C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op).hom.toAlgebra`, threaded
311	through the conclusion `@IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X, V) _ algebra` form (matching
312	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` signature exactly).
313	
314	**Explicit `@`-algebra binding is essential.** Mathlib's default
315	`algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean` L724)
316	fires only for the syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, *not* for arbitrary `V`
317	definitionally equal to `X.basicOpen f`. The iter-058 plan-agent probe-confirmed that
318	stating this theorem with *inferred* algebra fails with
319	`failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra Γ(C.left, U) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h …)`.
320	Threading the algebra structure as part of the conclusion type — matching Mathlib's
321	signature exactly — sidesteps the synthesis attempt.
322	
323	This is the **fourth and final** thin foundational scaffolding helper on the affine
324	basic-open identification side. The iter-060+ basic-open Koszul acyclicity argument
325	(per the iter-059 analogy report at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, Branch A
326	substep 2) chains iter-057's equality + iter-058's inclusion + iter-059's localization
327	+ `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean`)
328	to identify each Čech-cochain factor `F(⋂_k 𝒰(x k))` with the cover-of-`D(f)` Čech complex
329	on localised modules. -/
330	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization
331	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
332	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
333	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
334	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)))
335	      Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) _
336	      ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE
337	        (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h)).op).hom.toAlgebra) :=
338	  hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen _
339	    (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h))
340	    (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod (C := C) (U := U) s t h)
341	
342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec
392	
393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
413	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
414	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
415	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
416	schemes in our setting.
417	
418	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
419	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
420	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
421	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
422	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
423	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
424	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
425	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
426	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
428	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
429	
430	The structural skeleton is:
431	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
432	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
433	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
434	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
435	
436	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
437	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
438	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
439	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
440	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
441	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
442	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
443	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
444	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
445	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
446	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
447	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
448	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
449	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
450	
451	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
452	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
453	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
454	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
455	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
456	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
457	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
458	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
459	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
460	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
461	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
462	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
463	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
464	    -- The goal is now
465	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
466	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
467	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
468	    --
469	    -- ===========================================================================
470	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
471	    -- ===========================================================================
472	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
473	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
474	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
475	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
476	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
477	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
478	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
479	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
480	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
481	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
482	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
483	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
484	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
485	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
486	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
487	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
488	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
489	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
490	      intro f
491	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
492	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
493	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
494	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
495	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
496	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
497	    -- ===========================================================================
498	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
499	    -- ===========================================================================
500	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
501	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
502	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
503	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
504	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
505	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
506	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
507	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
508	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
509	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
510	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
511	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:

    (h : mk I f ⟶ mk J g) (Δ : SimplexCategory) (j : Δ ⟶ I) :
    (mk I f).cech.obj Δ ⟶ (mk J g).cech.obj Δ :=
  (cechFunctor.map h).app Δ

-- We want to know what test_g does to the f and phi components
#check cechFunctor.map

        simp only [π, NatTrans.hcomp_app, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,

import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Products
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.TransferInstance

open CategoryTheory Limits ModuleCat

universe u v

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {ι : Type v} (M : ι → ModuleCat.{max v u} k)
  [HasProduct M]

noncomputable def productLinearEquiv : ((∏ᶜ M) : ModuleCat k) ≃ₗ[k] (∀ i, (M i : Type _)) where
  toFun := (piIsoPi M).hom.hom
  invFun := (piIsoPi M).inv.hom
  left_inv x := by
    have h := (piIsoPi M).hom_inv_id
    exact DFunLike.congr_fun (congr_arg Hom.hom h) x
  right_inv x := by
    have h := (piIsoPi M).inv_hom_id
    exact DFunLike.congr_fun (congr_arg Hom.hom h) x
  map_add' x y := by
    exact (piIsoPi M).hom.hom.map_add x y
  map_smul' c x := by
    exact (piIsoPi M).hom.hom.map_smul c x

-- Now try transporting a module structure
variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [(i : ι) → Module R (M i)]

@[reducible]
noncomputable def productModuleR : Module R ((∏ᶜ M) : ModuleCat k) :=
  AddEquiv.module R (productLinearEquiv M).toAddEquiv

#check productModuleR

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).

45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +

362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec

396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an

398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`

404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}

408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2021 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou, Adam Topaz, Johan Commelin

8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Additive
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.MooreComplex
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Fin
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Idempotents.FunctorCategories
12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FunctorCategory.EpiMono
13	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Opposite
14	
15	/-!
16	
17	# The alternating face map complex of a simplicial object in a preadditive category
18	
19	We construct the alternating face map complex, as a
20	functor `alternatingFaceMapComplex : SimplicialObject C ⥤ ChainComplex C ℕ`
21	for any preadditive category `C`. For any simplicial object `X` in `C`,
22	this is the homological complex `... → X_2 → X_1 → X_0`
23	where the differentials are alternating sums of faces.
24	
25	The dual version `alternatingCofaceMapComplex : CosimplicialObject C ⥤ CochainComplex C ℕ`
26	is also constructed.
27	
28	We also construct the natural transformation
29	`inclusionOfMooreComplex : normalizedMooreComplex A ⟶ alternatingFaceMapComplex A`
30	when `A` is an abelian category.
31	
32	## References
33	* https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/0194
34	* https://ncatlab.org/nlab/show/Moore+complex
35	
36	-/
37	
38	@[expose] public section
39	
40	
41	open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits CategoryTheory.Subobject
42	
43	open CategoryTheory.Preadditive CategoryTheory.Category CategoryTheory.Idempotents
44	
45	open Opposite
46	
47	open Simplicial
48	
49	noncomputable section
50	
51	namespace AlgebraicTopology
52	
53	namespace AlternatingFaceMapComplex
54	
55	/-!
56	## Construction of the alternating face map complex
57	-/
58	
59	
60	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] [Preadditive C]
61	variable (X : SimplicialObject C)
62	variable (Y : SimplicialObject C)
63	
64	/-- The differential on the alternating face map complex is the alternate
65	sum of the face maps -/
66	@[simp]
67	def objD (n : ℕ) : X _⦋n + 1⦌ ⟶ X _⦋n⦌ :=
68	  ∑ i : Fin (n + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • X.δ i
69	
70	/-!
71	## The chain complex relation `d ≫ d`
72	-/
73	
74	theorem d_squared (n : ℕ) : objD X (n + 1) ≫ objD X n = 0 := by
75	  -- we start by expanding d ≫ d as a double sum
76	  dsimp
77	  simp only [comp_sum, sum_comp, ← Finset.sum_product']
78	  -- then, we decompose the index set P into a subset S and its complement Sᶜ
79	  let P := Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 3)
80	  let S : Finset P := {ij : P | (ij.2 : ℕ) ≤ (ij.1 : ℕ)}
81	  rw [Finset.univ_product_univ, ← Finset.sum_add_sum_compl S, ← eq_neg_iff_add_eq_zero,
82	    ← Finset.sum_neg_distrib]
83	  /- we are reduced to showing that two sums are equal, and this is obtained
84	    by constructing a bijection φ : S -> Sᶜ, which maps (i,j) to (j,i+1),
85	    and by comparing the terms -/
86	  let φ : ∀ ij : P, ij ∈ S → P := fun ij hij =>
87	    (Fin.castLT ij.2 (lt_of_le_of_lt (Finset.mem_filter.mp hij).right (Fin.is_lt ij.1)), ij.1.succ)
88	  apply Finset.sum_bij φ
89	  · -- φ(S) is contained in Sᶜ
90	    intro ij hij
91	    simp_rw [S, φ, Finset.compl_filter, Finset.mem_filter_univ, Fin.val_succ,
92	      Fin.val_castLT] at hij ⊢
93	    lia
94	  · -- φ : S → Sᶜ is injective
95	    rintro ⟨i, j⟩ hij ⟨i', j'⟩ hij' h
96	    rw [Prod.mk_inj]
97	    exact ⟨by simpa [φ] using congr_arg Prod.snd h,
98	      by simpa [φ, Fin.castSucc_castLT] using congr_arg Fin.castSucc (congr_arg Prod.fst h)⟩
99	  · -- φ : S → Sᶜ is surjective
100	    rintro ⟨i', j'⟩ hij'

Great progress! The first `simp` (with no arguments) actually made significant progress! The goal went from:

```
⊢ 𝟙 (...) ≫ ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex (ModuleCat k)).map (...)).f i =
    Pi.lift fun b => Pi.π ... (g_FC.f ∘ b)
```

to:

```
⊢ (Pi.lift fun i_1 =>
      Pi.π (fun i_2 => (toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ basicOpenCover s ∘ i_2)))
          ((g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })).f i_1) ≫
        (toModuleKSheaf C).obj.map ((g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })).φ i_1).op) =
    Pi.lift fun b => Pi.π (fun i_1 => (toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ basicOpenCover s ∘ i_1))) (g_FC.f ∘ b)
```

Now I need to simplify the left-hand side further. Recall that `g_simp = FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor.map g_FC`. And `g_FC` is defined as:
```lean
{ f := fun j => ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩
  φ := fun _ => 𝟙 _ }
```

So `g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })` is a morphism in `FormalCoproduct` from `cech_s₀.obj (Opposite.op { len := i })` to `cech_s.obj (Opposite.op { len := i })`.

The `.f` field of this morphism gives a function on indices `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀ → Fin (i+1) → s`. And `.φ` gives the morphisms between the objects.

Since `g_FC.φ x = 𝟙 _`, we have `(g_simp.app ...).φ i_1 = 𝟙 _`. So the composition `Pi.π _ _ ≫ (toModuleKSheaf C).obj.map (𝟙 _).op` should simplify to just `Pi.π _ _` because `(𝟙 _).op = 𝟙 _` and `F.map 𝟙 = 𝟙`.

Also, `(g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })).f i_1` should equal `g_FC.f ∘ i_1`.

Let me verify this by looking at the `cechFunctor` definition. `cechFunctor` maps morphisms of `FormalCoproduct` to natural transformations of simplicial objects. At a simplicial object level `n`, the component should have `.f` equal to precomposition with the index map.

So the remaining goal should be provable with simp lemmas about `cechFunctor`, `evalOp`, and `g_FC`.

Let me try `simp` with more specific lemmas.

`powerMap` has fields:
- `.f i = g_FC.f ∘ i`
- `.φ i = Pi.map (fun a => g_FC.φ (i a))`

Since `g_FC.φ x = 𝟙 _`, we have `.φ i = Pi.map (fun a => 𝟙 _) = 𝟙 _` (by `Pi.map_id`).

And `.f i = g_FC.f ∘ i`.

Now, `g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })` is the component of the natural transformation `g_simp` at the object `Opposite.op { len := i }` of `SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ`. Since `g_simp = cechFunctor.map g_FC`, and `cechFunctor` maps from `FormalCoproduct` to `SimplicialObject`, the natural transformation's component at `{ len := i }` (in `SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ`) is `powerMap g_FC (Fin (i+1))`.

Wait, `cech` is defined as:
```lean
noncomputable def cech (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
  obj n := U.power (ToType n.unop)
  map f := U.mapPower f.unop.toOrderHom.toFun
```

So `cech` is a `SimplicialObject`, which is `SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ FormalCoproduct C`.

And `cechFunctor.map g_FC` is a natural transformation `cech_s₀ ⟶ cech_s`. Its `.app` at `n : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ` is `powerMap g_FC (ToType n.unop)`.

For `n = op { len := i }`, `n.unop = { len := i }`, and `ToType { len := i } = Fin (i+1)`.

So `g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i }) = powerMap g_FC (Fin (i+1))`.

Therefore:
- `(g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })).f i_1 = (powerMap g_FC (Fin (i+1))).f i_1 = g_FC.f ∘ i_1`
- `(g_simp.app (Opposite.op { len := i })).φ i_1 = (powerMap g_FC (Fin (i+1))).φ i_1 = Pi.map (fun a => g_FC.φ (i_1 a)) = Pi.map (fun a => 𝟙 _) = 𝟙 _`

For `?m a` to match, `?m` would need to be `basicOpenCover (↑s₀) ∘ i_1`, but `i_1` is a bound variable of type `Fin (i + 1) → ↑s₀`. And `a` is of type `Fin (i + 1)`. Wait, is `Pi.lift` injective? In a category with products, `Pi.lift` is defined by a universal property. Two morphisms `f, g : X → ∏ Y_i` are equal if and only if `Pi.π i ≫ f = Pi.π i ≫ g` for all `i`. But here we have `Pi.lift fun i => ...` and we want to show two such lifts are equal. Explanation: The rewrite tactic rewrites an expression 'e' using an equality 'a = b' by the following process. First, it looks for all 'a' in 'e'. Second, it tries to abstract these occurrences of 'a' to create a function 'm := fun _a => ...', called the *motive*, with the property that 'm a' is definitionally equal to 'e'. Third, we observe that 'congrArg' implies that 'm a = m b', which can be used with lemmas such as 'Eq.mpr' to change the goal. However, if 'e' depends on specific properties of 'a', then the motive 'm' might not typecheck.

Possible solutions: use rewrite's 'occs' configuration option to limit which occurrences are rewritten, or use 'simp' or 'conv' mode, which have strategies for certain kinds of dependencies (these tactics can handle proofs and 'Decidable' instances whose types depend on the rewritten term, and 'simp' can apply user-defined '@[congr]' theorems as well).

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2023 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Additive
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.Exact
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.Preadditive
11	public import Mathlib.Tactic.NormNum
12	
13	/-!
14	# The short complexes attached to homological complexes
15	
16	In this file, we define a functor
17	`shortComplexFunctor C c i : HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C`.
18	By definition, the image of a homological complex `K` by this functor
19	is the short complex `K.X (c.prev i) ⟶ K.X i ⟶ K.X (c.next i)`.
20	
21	The homology `K.homology i` of a homological complex `K` in degree `i` is defined as
22	the homology of the short complex `(shortComplexFunctor C c i).obj K`, which can be
23	abbreviated as `K.sc i`.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Category Limits
30	
31	namespace HomologicalComplex
32	
33	variable (C : Type*) [Category* C] [HasZeroMorphisms C] {ι : Type*} (c : ComplexShape ι)
34	
35	/-- The functor `HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C` which sends a homological
36	complex `K` to the short complex `K.X i ⟶ K.X j ⟶ K.X k` for arbitrary indices `i`, `j` and `k`. -/
37	@[simps]
38	def shortComplexFunctor' (i j k : ι) : HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C where
39	  obj K := ShortComplex.mk (K.d i j) (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k)
40	  map f :=
41	    { τ₁ := f.f i
42	      τ₂ := f.f j
43	      τ₃ := f.f k }
44	
45	/-- The functor `HomologicalComplex C c ⥤ ShortComplex C` which sends a homological
46	complex `K` to the short complex `K.X (c.prev i) ⟶ K.X i ⟶ K.X (c.next i)`. -/
47	@[simps!]
48	noncomputable def shortComplexFunctor (i : ι) :=
49	  shortComplexFunctor' C c (c.prev i) i (c.next i)
50	
51	/-- The natural isomorphism `shortComplexFunctor C c j ≅ shortComplexFunctor' C c i j k`
52	when `c.prev j = i` and `c.next j = k`. -/
53	@[simps!]
54	noncomputable def natIsoSc' (i j k : ι) (hi : c.prev j = i) (hk : c.next j = k) :
55	    shortComplexFunctor C c j ≅ shortComplexFunctor' C c i j k :=
56	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun K => ShortComplex.isoMk (K.XIsoOfEq hi) (Iso.refl _) (K.XIsoOfEq hk)
57	    (by simp) (by simp)) (by cat_disch)
58	
59	variable {C c}

6	module

9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech
10	
11	/-!
12	# Cech cohomology
13	
14	Given a family of objects `U : ι → C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
15	we define a Cech complex functor
16	`cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf
17	`P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
18	complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι`,
19	of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe w t v v' u u'
26	
27	namespace CategoryTheory
28	
29	open Limits
30	
31	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]
32	
33	namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct
34	
35	open Opposite
36	
37	variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))
38	
39	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
40	functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
41	cosimplicial object which sends `⦋n⦌` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
42	@[simps!]
43	noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
44	    (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A :=
45	  evalOp.{w} C A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp
46	
47	variable [Preadditive A]
48	
49	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
50	functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
51	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
52	@[simps!]
53	noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
54	  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A
55	
56	end Limits.FormalCoproduct
57	
58	variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {ι : Type w} (U : ι → C)
59	
60	/-- Given a family of objects `U : ι → C`, this is the Cech complex functor
61	`(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
62	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
63	indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) → ι`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
64	objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
65	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
66	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
67	
68	end CategoryTheory

```lean
instance algebra_section_section_basicOpen {X : Scheme} {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) :
    Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) :=
  (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE <| X.basicOpen_le f : _ ⟶ U).op).hom.toAlgebra
```

This is exactly what we need! For any section `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`, `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` has an `Algebra Γ(X, U)` instance.

Now, what about the intersection of basic opens? For `W = ⨅_{a} basicOpen (i a)`, we need `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, W)`.

Since each `basicOpen (i a) ≤ U` (by `basicOpen_le`), the intersection `W` is also `≤ U`. We need an algebra instance for the restriction to `W`.

For `W ≤ V ≤ U`, if we have `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` and `Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(X, W)`, then `Γ(X, W)` is also a `Γ(X, U)`-algebra via composition. But does Lean have this instance automatically?

Actually, `Algebra` is transitive in some sense. If `A` is an `R`-algebra and `B` is an `A`-algebra, then `B` is an `R`-algebra via `algebraMap R A` composed with `algebraMap A B`. Mathlib has `Algebra.comap` for this.

```lean
instance algebra_section_section_basicOpen {X : Scheme} {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) :
```

15	/-!
16	# Local properties about linear maps
17	
18	In this file, we show that
19	injectivity, surjectivity, bijectivity and exactness of linear maps are local properties.
20	More precisely, we show that these can be checked at maximal ideals and on standard covers.
21	-/
22	
23	public section
24	
25	open Submodule LocalizedModule Ideal LinearMap
26	
27	section isLocalized_maximal
28	
29	open IsLocalizedModule

11	/-!
12	# The full subcategory associated to a property of objects
13	
14	Given a category `C` and `P : ObjectProperty C`, we define
15	a category structure on the type `P.FullSubcategory`
16	of objects in `C` satisfying `P`.
17	
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	universe v v' u u'
23	
24	namespace CategoryTheory
25	
26	namespace ObjectProperty
27	
28	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
29	
30	section
31	
32	variable (P : ObjectProperty C)
33	
34	/--
35	A subtype-like structure for full subcategories. Morphisms just ignore the property. We don't use
36	actual subtypes since the simp-normal form `↑X` of `X.val` does not work well for full
37	subcategories. -/
38	@[ext, stacks 001D "We do not define 'strictly full' subcategories."]
39	structure FullSubcategory where
40	  /-- The category of which this is a full subcategory -/
41	  obj : C
42	  /-- The predicate satisfied by all objects in this subcategory -/
43	  property : P obj
44	
45	instance FullSubcategory.category : Category.{v} P.FullSubcategory :=
46	  inferInstanceAs (Category (InducedCategory _ FullSubcategory.obj))
47	
48	instance [P.Nonempty] : Nonempty P.FullSubcategory :=
49	  Nonempty.intro ⟨P.arbitrary, P.prop_arbitrary⟩
50	
51	@[ext]
52	lemma hom_ext {X Y : P.FullSubcategory} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h : f.hom = g.hom) : f = g :=
53	  InducedCategory.hom_ext h
54	
55	/-- The forgetful functor from a full subcategory into the original category
56	("forgetting" the condition).
57	-/
58	def ι : P.FullSubcategory ⥤ C :=
59	  inducedFunctor FullSubcategory.obj
